question: Identify the agricultural college that was ultimately located in the town that beat the county seat of the Washington county with the lowest 2020 census population among those counties that (1) had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22, (2) had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in the 2021-22 school year, and (3) were not the most populous county in Washington. Only report the college name. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/report-card-enrollment-2018-19-school-year/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

filtered = df[(df["OrganizationLevel"] == "District") & (df["GradeLevel"] == "All Grades")]
county = filtered.groupby("County", as_index=False)[["Low-Income", "All Students"]].sum()
county["Low-Income%"] = county["Low-Income"] / county["All Students"] * 100
answer = county[(county["Low-Income%"] >= 32) & (county["Low-Income%"] <= 40)].sort_values("County")["County"].tolist()
print(answer)
1.subquestion: Which Washington counties had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22? (Filter: OrganizationLevel=District; GradeLevel=All Grades; group by County; Low-Income% = Low-Income / All Students * 100; keep 32-40%.)

import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/report-card-enrollment-2021-22-school-year/files/rows.txt"

county["Low-Income%"] = county["Low-Income"] / county["All Students"] * 100

4.subquestion: Which Washington counties had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22? (Filter: OrganizationLevel=District; GradeLevel=All Grades; group by County; Low-Income% = Low-Income / All Students * 100; keep 32-40%.)

5.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/report-card-assessment-data-2021-22-school-year/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

filtered = df[
    (df["OrganizationLevel"] == "District")
    & (df["StudentGroup"] == "All Students")
    & (df["GradeLevel"] == "All Grades")
    & (df["TestSubject"] == "Math")
    & (df["County"].isin(["King", "Kitsap", "San Juan", "Whitman"]))
].copy()
filtered["PercentMetStandard"] = pd.to_numeric(
    filtered["PercentMetStandard"].astype(str).str.rstrip("%"), errors="coerce"
)
county = filtered.groupby("County", as_index=False)["PercentMetStandard"].mean()
answer = county[county["PercentMetStandard"] > 40].sort_values("County")["County"].tolist()
print(answer)
5.subquestion: Among {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman} (from hop 1), which counties had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in 2021-22? (Filter: OrganizationLevel=District; StudentGroup=All Students; GradeLevel=All Grades; TestSubject=Math; compute unweighted mean of PercentMetStandard by County; keep >40%.)

6.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for King County, Washington, 'The population was 2,269,675 in the 2020 census, making it the most populous county in Washington.'
6.subquestion: Among {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman} (from hop 1), which counties are NOT the most populous in Washington?

7.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Kitsap County, Washington, 'As of the 2020 census, its population was 275,611.'
7.subquestion: Among {Kitsap, Whitman} (from hop 2), which county had the lower 2020 census population?

8.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Whitman County, Washington, 'As of the 2020 census, the population was 47,973.' Also, 'The county seat is Colfax.'
8.subquestion: Among {Kitsap, Whitman} (from hop 2), which county had the lower 2020 census population?
8.answer: Whitman had population 47,973 (lower than Kitsap's 275,611), county seat is Colfax

9.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Colfax, Washington, 'In 1889-90, the town vied with several other finalists to become the site of a new state agricultural college, present-day Washington State University. The honor ultimately fell to nearby Pullman.'
9.subquestion: What agricultural college did Colfax (from hop 3) compete to host, and which town won the site selection?
9.answer: Washington State University, Pullman won the competition

Node 2: 2019-20 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}

Result: Whitman County, county seat = Colfax
